name: Byron, Hunter
description: Removed with his parents to Willamette valley, Oregon, at the age of four. Seven years later moved to Latah county, Idaho, where he attended the district schools. Was graduated from the State Normal School at Monmouth, Oregon, in 1894, being one of ten in a class of fifty-two that delivered orations on Commencement Day. Represented the Washington as orator in the annual entertainment of literary societies, Commencement week, 1897.

name: Anna, M. Ellis
description: Attended the public schools in her early youth; also attended the common schools at Holton, Kansas; taught school in Latah county, Idaho, at the age of fifteen. Was graduated from the preparatory department in June, 1894. Gave piano recital during Commencement week in June, 1898. Will finish the course in music in June, 1899.

name: Mccroskey, Virgil Talmage
major: B. S. in Economic Science and History
description: Moved out west to grow up with the country in August, 1879; attended district school, and entered the public schools at Colfax in 1888; can tell you all about the early history of the college, especially the potato patch and the rotten egg. Was graduated from the Preparatory Department in June, 1894. Was graduated from the School of Pharmacy with degree of Ph. G. in June, 1898; successfully passed the examinations before the State Board of Pharmacy at Spokane, in April, 1898.

name: Lobaugh, Dora Orlette
description: Completed the course in the Pullman public school in May, 1894; finished the Preparatory course in June, 1895; Thesis: "Shakespeare's Fools and Clowns."

name: Van Doren, W. Seaton
high school: High School at Brookings, South Dakota, and later the Academy at Le Roy, New York
clubs: Represented the Columbian as orator in spring entertainment
description: Entered the Washington Agricultural College at its opening in January, 1892, when the "crib" was the only building on the hill; member of the first class that was graduated from the Preparatory department; Thesis: "Landscaping the College Campus."

name: Malone, Lora Dell
description: Attended District school No. 33 until twelve years of age, when she entered the Pullman public school; completed the Preparatory Course in June, 1895; Thesis: "A Synopsis of the Uredineal and Ustilagineal of Washington."

name: Hunting, Samuel E.
high school: Endicott school, and later the public schools of Colfax; Baptist College at Colfax
description: Taught in the district schools of this county for three terms; came to the Washington Agricultural College and School of Science in November, 1894; Thesis: "A Design for a Heat, Light and Power Plant for the College."

name: Taylor, Mabel
clubs: Represented the Columbian Society as declaimer
description: Removed to Portland, Ore., in 1886, and a year later to Pullman, Wash; attended Pullman public school; entered the Washington Agricultural College and School of Science in September, 1894; completed the Preparatory Course in June, 1895; Thesis: "Determination of the Acceleration of Gravity."
